title: Pundarikakshan Perumal Temple
tagline: Pundarikakshan Perumal Temple — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: The Pundarikakshan Perumal Temple or Thiruvellarai Temple in Thiruvellarai, a village in the outskirts of Tiruchirappalli in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu, is dedicated to t
overview.description: The Pundarikakshan Perumal Temple or Thiruvellarai Temple in Thiruvellarai, a village in the outskirts of Tiruchirappalli in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu, is dedicated to the Hindu god Vishnu. Constructed in the Dravidian style of architecture, the temple is glorified in the Naalayira Divya Prabandham, the early medieval Tamil canon of the Alvar saints from the 6th–9th centuries CE. It is one of the 108 Divya Desams dedicated to Vishnu, who is worshipped as Pundarikakshan and his consort Lakshmi as Pankajavalli. According to legends, the temple is said to have been built by Sibi Chakra

topPlaces.name: Battle of Seringham
topPlaces.description: The Battle of Seringham was fought on the island of Srirangam between 1,000 troops of the British East India Company commanded by Stringer Lawrence and a confederacy of French East India Company troops and Chanda Sahib.

topPlaces.name: Marungapuri taluk
topPlaces.description: Marungapuri Taluk is a taluk of Tiruchirappalli district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It was split from Manapparai taluk in 2013.

topPlaces.name: Ponnampatti
topPlaces.description: Ponnampatti is a panchayat town in Tiruchirappalli district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu.

topPlaces.name: Kalpalayathanpatti
topPlaces.description: Kalpalayathanpatti is a village in Tiruchirappalli district, Tamil Nadu, India. This village has around 5000 people, many of whom work in agriculture and construction works.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Pundarikakshan Perumal Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Pundarikakshan Perumal Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Pundarikakshan Perumal Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Tiruchirappalli International Airport (45 km) and the nearest railway station is Tiruchirappalli Junction (46 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Pundarikakshan Perumal Temple?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Pundarikakshan Perumal Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Pundarikakshan Perumal Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Pundarikakshan Perumal Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Pundarikakshan Perumal Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Battle of Seringham, Marungapuri taluk, Ponnampatti, Kalpalayathanpatti.
